subject: ap-government
title: Quantitative Analysis — Cabinet Diversity by President, 1981–2017
stimulus: Source: Georgia Public Broadcasting News, 2017. Bar graphs showing Cabinet Diversity by President from 1981 to 2017, displaying the percentage of women and racial/ethnic minorities serving in the Cabinet under each president (Reagan, G.H.W. Bush, Clinton, G.W. Bush, and Obama).

parts.prompt: Identify the president who had the highest percentage of women and racial/ethnic minorities serving in the Cabinet.

scoring_points.description: Identify the president who had the highest percentage of women and racial/ethnic minorities serving in the Cabinet.

required_elements: President Obama had the highest percentage of women and racial/ethnic minorities serving in his Cabinet.
alternatives.correct_example: President Obama had the highest percentage of women and racial/ethnic minorities serving in his Cabinet.

parts.prompt: Describe a pattern in the percentage of racial and ethnic minorities serving in Cabinet positions.
parts.scoring_notes: Accept any accurate pattern description supported by the data. The response must be a pattern (trend or relationship), not just a single data point.

scoring_points.description: Describe a pattern in the percentage of racial and ethnic minorities serving in Cabinet positions.

required_elements: Democratic presidents tend to appoint more racial and ethnic minorities to Cabinet positions than Republican presidents.
alternatives.correct_example: Democratic presidents tend to appoint more racial and ethnic minorities to Cabinet positions than Republican presidents.

required_elements: Recent presidents are appointing more racial and ethnic minorities to Cabinet positions, regardless of party.
alternatives.correct_example: Recent presidents are appointing more racial and ethnic minorities to Cabinet positions, regardless of party.

parts.prompt: Draw a conclusion about the political parties and the diversity of Cabinet appointments as shown in the bar graphs.
parts.scoring_notes: Accept any plausible conclusion supported by the data that makes an inference beyond mere description. The response must go beyond describing what the data show to drawing a broader conclusion.

scoring_points.description: Draw a conclusion about the political parties and the diversity of Cabinet appointments as shown in the bar graphs.

required_elements: Democratic presidents have appointed more women and minorities because that reflects the demographics of their voters.
alternatives.correct_example: Democratic presidents have appointed more women and minorities because that reflects the demographics of their voters.

required_elements: Republican presidents in the future could appoint more women and minorities to Cabinet level positions in an attempt to appeal to a wider group of voters.
alternatives.correct_example: Republican presidents in the future could appoint more women and minorities to Cabinet level positions in an attempt to appeal to a wider group of voters.

parts.prompt: Explain how interest groups can influence presidential Cabinet appointments as shown in the bar graphs.
parts.scoring_notes: The response must explain a specific mechanism by which interest groups could influence appointments. Vague statements about 'pressuring' the president without describing a specific method do not earn the point.

scoring_points.description: Explain how interest groups can influence presidential Cabinet appointments as shown in the bar graphs.

required_elements: Interest groups can use media campaigns to pressure the president to make Cabinet appointments that reflect the interests of the group.
alternatives.correct_example: Interest groups can use media campaigns to pressure the president to make Cabinet appointments that reflect the interests of the group.

required_elements: Interest groups can use media campaigns to pressure the Senate to confirm or not confirm appointments based on how they reflect the interests of the group.
alternatives.correct_example: Interest groups can use media campaigns to pressure the Senate to confirm or not confirm appointments based on how they reflect the interests of the group.

required_elements: Interest groups can lobby members of the Senate to vote for or against a presidential Cabinet nominee.
alternatives.correct_example: Interest groups can lobby members of the Senate to vote for or against a presidential Cabinet nominee.
